1. Look at the template project structure
2. Read the affected files to understand what changes need to be made
3. Create a JavaParser transformation to fix these issues

Let me explore the template project and read the affected PGS files.

Can be negative 436: * @param n the number of random points to generate 437: * @param seed number used to initialize the underlying pseudorandom 438: * number generator 439: * @return a list of PVector objects representing the (x, y) coordinates of the 440: * random points 441: * @see #ring(double, double, double, double, double, int) non-seeded ring() 442: */ 443: public static List ring(double centerX, double centerY, double innerRadius, double outerRadius, double maxAngle, int n, 444: long seed) { 445: final SplittableRandom random = new SplittableRandom(seed); 446: final List points = new ArrayList<>(n); 447: if (maxAngle == 0) { 448: maxAngle = Double.MIN_VALUE; 449: } 450: for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { 451: double randomAngle = (maxAngle < 0 ? -1 : 1) * random.nextDouble(Math.abs(maxAngle)); 452: double randomRadius = random.nextDouble(innerRadius, outerRadius); 453: double x = -Math.sin(randomAngle) * randomRadius; 454: double y = Math.cos(randomAngle) * randomRadius; 455: 456: points.add(new PVector((float) (x + centerX), (float) (y + centerY))); 457: } 458: return points; 459: } 460: 461: /** 462: * Generates a set of random points (constrained within a rectangular region) 463: * via Poisson Disk Sampling. 464: *

This distribution is also 489: * described as blue noise. 490: * 491: * @param xMin x-coordinate of boundary minimum 492: * @param yMin y-coordinate of boundary minimum 493: * @param xMax x-coordinate of boundary maximum 494: * @param yMax y-coordinate of boundary maximum 495: * @param minDist minimum euclidean distance between any two points 496: * @param seed number used to initialize the underlying pseudorandom number 497: * generator 498: * @return 499: * @see #poisson(double, double, double, double, double) non-seeded poisson() 500: */ 501: public static List poisson(double xMin, double yMin, double xMax, double yMax, double minDist, long seed) { 502: final PoissonDistributionJRUS pd = new PoissonDistributionJRUS(seed); 503: return pd.generate(xMin, yMin, xMax, yMax, minDist); 504: } 505: 506: /** 507: * Generates a poisson point set having N points constrained within a 508: * rectangular region. 509: *

526: return pd.generate(xMin, yMin, xMax, yMax, n); 527: } 528: 529: /** 530: * Generates a set of points arranged in a phyllotaxis pattern (an arrangement 531: * similar to the florets in the head of a sunflower), using the golden ratio 532: * (the most irrational number) to position points with the least possible 533: * aliasing (which is arguably the "best" arrangement). 534: * 535: * @param centerX x coordinate of the center of the point set 536: * @param centerY y coordinate of the center of the point set 537: * @param n number of points to generate 538: * @param radius radius of circular phyllotaxis extent (max distance of a point 539: * from the center position) 540: * @return 541: */ 542: public static List phyllotaxis(double centerX, double centerY, int n, double radius) { 543: return phyllotaxis(centerX, centerY, n, radius, 2 * Math.PI - GOLDEN_ANGLE); 544: } 545: 546: /** 547: * Generates a set of points arranged in a phyllotaxis pattern (an arrangement 548: * similar to the florets in the head of a sunflower), using a user-defined 549: * theta. 550: * 551: * @param centerX x coordinate of the center of the point set 552: * @param centerY y coordinate of the center of the point set 553: * @param n number of points to generate 554: * @param radius radius of circular phyllotaxis extent (max distance of a point 555: * from the center position) 556: * @param theta angle (in radians) to turn after each point placement 557: * @return 558: */ 559: public static List phyllotaxis(double centerX, double centerY, int n, double radius, double theta) { 560: final double fillArea = radius * radius * Math.PI; // calculate area to be filled 561: final double circleSpace = (fillArea / n); // area per circle 562: final double fudge = 0.7; // Fudge factor: breathing space between circles 563: final float circleRadius = (float) (Math.sqrt(circleSpace / Math.PI) * fudge); 564: 565: float cumArea = 0; // cumulative circle area 566: 567: final List outList = new ArrayList<>(); 568: for (int i = 1; i <= n; ++i) { 569: final double angle = i * theta; // rotation per circle 570: cumArea += circleSpace; // add sm_area to cum_area every loop 571: 572: final double spiralR = Math.sqrt(cumArea / Math.PI); // expansion of spiral (distance of circle) per loop 573: 574: float pX = (float) (centerX + Math.cos(angle) * spiralR); // spiral rotation of golden angle per loop on X 575: float pY = (float) (centerY + Math.sin(angle) * spiralR); // spiral rotation of golden angle per loop on Y 576: 577: outList.add(new PVector(pX, pY, circleRadius)); 578: } 579: return outList; 580: } 581: 582: /** 583: * Generates a set of deterministic stratified points (bounded by a rectangle) 584: * from a low discrepancy sequence (LDS) based on an irrational number (the 585: * plastic constant). 586: *

592: * Low discrepancy sequences are deterministic (not randomized) number sequences 593: * that are low discrepancy - meaning the points tend not to clump together and 594: * leave holes; the resulting point set is more evenly spaced than a simple 595: * random distribution but less regular than lattices. 596: * 597: * @param xMin x-coordinate of boundary minimum 598: * @param yMin y-coordinate of boundary minimum 599: * @param xMax x-coordinate of boundary maximum 600: * @param yMax y-coordinate of boundary maximum 601: * @param n number of points to generate 602: * @return 603: */ 604: public static List plasticLDS(double xMin, double yMin, double xMax, double yMax, int n) { 605: // https://github.com/Atrix256/SampleZoo/blob/master/src/families/_2d/samples/irrational_numbers/irrational_numbers.cpp 606: final double w = xMax - xMin; 607: final double h = yMax - yMin; 608: final double p = 1.32471795724474602596; // plastic constant 609: final double a1 = 1.0 / p; // inverse of plastic number 610: final double a2 = 1.0 / (p * p); 611: 612: final List points = new ArrayList<>(n); 613: for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { 614: final float x = (float) (((0.5 + a1 * i) % 1) * w + xMin); 615: final float y = (float) (((0.5 + a2 * i) % 1) * h + yMin); 616: points.add(new PVector(x, y)); 617: } 618: return points; 619: } 620: 621: /** 622: * Generates a set of deterministic stratified points (bounded by a rectangle) 623: * from a low discrepancy sequence (LDS) based on an irrational number. 627: * Low discrepancy sequences are deterministic (not randomized) number sequences 628: * that are low discrepancy - meaning the points tend not to clump together and 629: * leave holes; the resulting point set is more evenly spaced than a simple 630: * random distribution but less regular than lattices. 631: * 632: * @param xMin x-coordinate of boundary minimum 633: * @param yMin y-coordinate of boundary minimum 634: * @param xMax x-coordinate of boundary maximum 635: * @param yMax y-coordinate of boundary maximum 636: * @param n number of points to generate 637: * @return 638: * @see #plasticJitteredLDS(double, double, double, double, int, long) seeded 639: * irrationalJitteredLDS() 640: */ 641: public static List plasticJitteredLDS(double xMin, double yMin, double xMax, double yMax, int n) { 642: return plasticJitteredLDS(xMin, yMin, xMax, yMax, n, System.nanoTime()); 643: } 644: 645: /** 646: * Generates a set of deterministic stratified points (bounded by a rectangle) 647: * from a low discrepancy sequence (LDS) based on an irrational number. In this 648: * method, a random jitter is added to points to give the point set 649: * blue-noise-like properties. 650: *

651: * Low discrepancy sequences are deterministic (not randomized) number sequences 652: * that are low discrepancy - meaning the points tend not to clump together and 653: * leave holes; the resulting point set is more evenly spaced than a simple 654: * random distribution but less regular than lattices. 655: * 656: * @param xMin x-coordinate of boundary minimum 657: * @param yMin y-coordinate of boundary minimum 658: * @param xMax x-coordinate of boundary maximum 659: * @param yMax y-coordinate of boundary maximum 660: * @param n number of points to generate 661: * @param seed number used to initialize the underlying pseudorandom number 662: * generator 663: * @return 664: * @see #plasticJitteredLDS(double, double, double, double, int) non-seeded 665: * irrationalJitteredLDS() 666: */ 667: public static List plasticJitteredLDS(double xMin, double yMin, double xMax, double yMax, int n, long seed) { 668: // https://github.com/Atrix256/SampleZoo/blob/master/src/families/_2d/samples/irrational_numbers/irrational_numbers.cpp 669: final double w = xMax - xMin; 670: final double h = yMax - yMin; 671: 672: final SplittableRandom random = new SplittableRandom(seed); 673: final double p = 1.32471795724474602596; // plastic constant 674: final double a1 = 1.0 / p; 675: final double a2 = 1.0 / (p * p); 676: final double c_magicNumber = 0.732; 677: 678: final List points = new ArrayList<>(n); 679: for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { 680: final float x = (float) (((random.nextDouble() * c_magicNumber / Math.sqrt(i + 1d) + a1 * i) % 1) * w + xMin); 681: final float y = (float) (((random.nextDouble() * c_magicNumber / Math.sqrt(i + 1d) + a2 * i) % 1) * h + yMin); 682: points.add(new PVector(x, y)); 683: } 684: return points; 685: } 686: 687: /** 688: * Generates a set of deterministic stratified points (bounded by a rectangle) 689: * from a low discrepancy sequence (LDS) based on a Halton sequence. 690: *

691: * Low discrepancy sequences are deterministic (not randomized) number sequences 692: * that are low discrepancy - meaning the points tend not to clump together and 693: * leave holes; the resulting point set is more evenly spaced than a simple 694: * random distribution but less regular than lattices. 695: * 696: * @param xMin x-coordinate of boundary minimum 697: * @param yMin y-coordinate of boundary minimum 698: * @param xMax x-coordinate of boundary maximum 699: * @param yMax y-coordinate of boundary maximum 700: * @param n number of points to generate 701: * @return 702: */ 703: public static List haltonLDS(double xMin, double yMin, double xMax, double yMax, int n) { 704: final double w = xMax - xMin; 705: final double h = yMax - yMin; 706: float[][] values = new float[n][2]; 707: vanDerCorput(values, 2, 0, true, 0); 708: vanDerCorput(values, 3, 1, true, 0); 709: 710: final List points = new ArrayList<>(n); 711: for (float[] point : values) { 712: points.add(new PVector((float) (point[0] * w + xMin), (float) (point[1] * h + yMin))); 713: } 714: return points; 715: } 716: 717: /** 718: * Generates a set of deterministic stratified points (bounded by a rectangle) 719: * from a low discrepancy sequence (LDS) based on a Hammersley sequence. 720: *

721: * The Hammersley sequence in 2D is just the 1d Van Der Corput sequence on one 722: * axis, and regular sampling on the other axis. 723: *

724: * Low discrepancy sequences are deterministic (not randomized) number sequences 725: * that are low discrepancy - meaning the points tend not to clump together and 726: * leave holes; the resulting point set is more evenly spaced than a simple 727: * random distribution but less regular than lattices. 728: * 729: * @param xMin x-coordinate of boundary minimum 730: * @param yMin y-coordinate of boundary minimum 731: * @param xMax x-coordinate of boundary maximum 732: * @param yMax y-coordinate of boundary maximum 733: * @param n number of points to generate 734: * @return 735: */ 736: public static List hammersleyLDS(double xMin, double yMin, double xMax, double yMax, int n) { 737: final double w = xMax - xMin; 738: final double h = yMax - yMin; 739: 740: float[][] values = new float[n][2]; 741: vanDerCorput(values, 2, 0, false, 0); 742: 743: final float offset = 1.0f / (n * 2); 744: for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) { 745: values[i][1] = offset + (i / (float) n); 746: } 747: 748: final List points = new ArrayList<>(n); 749: for (float[] point : values) { 750: points.add(new PVector((float) (point[0] * w + xMin), (float) (point[1] * h + yMin))); 751: } 752: return points; 753: } 754: 755: /** 756: * Generates a set of random stratified points (bounded by a rectangle) based on 757: * the "N-Rooks" sampling pattern. 758: *

759: * N-Rooks is a sampling pattern where you treat the boundary as if it were a 760: * chess board. Every sampling position is a rook that could move horizontally 761: * or vertically, and should be placed such that none of these rooks could 762: * capture/"see" any of the other rooks. In other words, every column has a 763: * single sample point in it, and every row has a single sample point in it. 764: * 765: * @param xMin x-coordinate of boundary minimum 766: * @param yMin y-coordinate of boundary minimum 767: * @param xMax x-coordinate of boundary maximum 768: * @param yMax y-coordinate of boundary maximum 769: * @param n number of points to generate 770: * @return 771: * @see #nRooksLDS(double, double, double, double, int, long) 772: */ 773: public static List nRooksLDS(double xMin, double yMin, double xMax, double yMax, int n) { 774: return nRooksLDS(xMin, yMin, xMax, yMax, n, System.nanoTime()); 775: } 776: 777: /** 778: * Generates a set of random stratified points (bounded by a rectangle) based on 779: * the "N-Rooks" sampling pattern. 780: *

781: * N-Rooks is a sampling pattern where you treat the boundary as if it were a 782: * chess board. Every sampling position is a rook that could move horizontally 783: * or vertically, and should be placed such that none of these rooks could 784: * capture/"see" any of the other rooks. In other words, every column has a 785: * single sample point in it, and every row has a single sample point in it. 786: * 787: * @param xMin x-coordinate of boundary minimum 788: * @param yMin y-coordinate of boundary minimum 789: * @param xMax x-coordinate of boundary maximum 790: * @param yMax y-coordinate of boundary maximum 791: * @param n number of points to generate 792: * @param seed number used to initialize the underlying pseudorandom number 793: * generator 794: * @return 795: * @see #nRooksLDS(double, double, double, double, int) 796: */ 797: public static List nRooksLDS(double xMin, double yMin, double xMax, double yMax, int n, long seed) { 798: final double w = xMax - xMin; 799: final double h = yMax - yMin; 800: 801: final List rookPositions = IntStream.range(0, n).boxed().collect(Collectors.toList()); 802: Collections.shuffle(rookPositions, new XoRoShiRo128PlusRandom(seed)); 803: 804: final float offset = 1.0f / (n * 2); 805: 806: final List points = new ArrayList<>(n); 807: for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { 808: float x = offset + (rookPositions.get(i) / (float) n); 809: x *= w; 810: x += xMin; 811: float y = offset + i / (float) n; 812: y *= h; 813: y += yMin; 814: points.add(new PVector(x, y)); 815: } 816: 817: return points; 818: } 819: 820: /** 821: * Generates a 2D set of deterministic stratified points (bounded by a 822: * rectangle) from the Sobol low discrepancy sequence (LDS). 823: *

824: * A Sobol sequence is a low-discrepancy sequence with the property that for all 825: * values of N,its subsequence (x1, ... xN) has a low discrepancy. It can be 826: * used to generate pseudo-randompoints in a space S, which are 827: * equi-distributed. 828: * 829: * @param xMin x-coordinate of boundary minimum 830: * @param yMin y-coordinate of boundary minimum 831: * @param xMax x-coordinate of boundary maximum 832: * @param yMax y-coordinate of boundary maximum 833: * @param n number of points to generate 834: * @since 1.4.0 835: * @return 836: */ 837: public static List sobolLDS(double xMin, double yMin, double xMax, double yMax, int n) { 838: final double w = xMax - xMin; 839: final double h = yMax - yMin; 840: final int dimension = 2; 841: final int BITS = 52; 842: final double SCALE = FastMath.pow(2, BITS); 843: final long[][] direction = new long[dimension][BITS + 1]; 844: final long[] x = new long[dimension]; 845: final int[] m = new int[] { 0, 1 }; 846: final int a = 0; 847: final int s = m.length - 1; 848: 849: for (int i = 1; i <= BITS; i++) { 850: direction[0][i] = 1l << (BITS - i); 851: } 852: 853: // init direction vector 854: final int d = 1; 855: for (int i = 1; i <= s; i++) { 856: direction[d][i] = ((long) m[i]) << (BITS - i); 857: } 858: for (int i = s + 1; i <= BITS; i++) { 859: direction[d][i] = direction[d][i - s] ^ (direction[d][i - s] >> s); 860: for (int k = 1; k <= s - 1; k++) { 861: direction[d][i] ^= ((a >> (s - 1 - k)) & 1) * direction[d][i - k]; 862: } 863: } 864: 865: List output = new ArrayList<>(n); 866: for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) { 867: 868: // find the index c of the rightmost 0 869: int c = 1; 870: int value = i - 1; 871: while ((value & 1) == 1) { 872: value >>= 1; 873: c++; 874: } 875: 876: x[0] ^= direction[0][c]; 877: x[1] ^= direction[1][c]; 878: double vX = x[0] / SCALE; 879: vX *= w; 880: vX += xMin; 881: double vY = x[1] / SCALE; 882: vY *= h; 883: vY += yMin; 884: output.add(new PVector((float) vX, (float) vY)); 885: } 886: 887: return output; 888: } 889: 890: /** 891: * Computes the Euclidean minimum spanning tree (EMST) of a set of 892: * points. 893: *

894: * The EMST is a system of line segments, having only the given points as their 895: * endpoints, whose union includes all of the points in a connected set, and 896: * which has the minimum possible total length of any such system. 897: * 898: * @param points the set of points over which to compute the EMST 899: * @return a LINES PShape 900: * @since 1.3.0 901: */ 902: public static PShape minimumSpanningTree(List points) { 903: /* 904: * The Euclidean minimum spanning tree in a plane is a subgraph of the Delaunay 905: * triangulation. 906: */ 907: IIncrementalTin triangulation = PGS_Triangulation.delaunayTriangulationMesh(points); 908: SimpleGraph graph = PGS_Triangulation.toGraph(triangulation); 909: SpanningTreeAlgorithm st = new PrimMinimumSpanningTree<>(graph); // faster than kruskal algorithm 910: return PGS_SegmentSet.toPShape(st.getSpanningTree().getEdges()); 911: } 912: 913: /** 914: * Computes the hilbert index of a coordinate on a hilbert curve of order n. 915: */ 916: private static int xy2Hilbert(final int px, final int py, final int n) { 917: int i, xi, yi; 918: int s, temp; 919: 920: int x = px; 921: int y = py; 922: s = 0; // Initialize. 923: for (i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) { 924: xi = (x >> i) & 1; // Get bit i of x. 925: yi = (y >> i) & 1; // Get bit i of y. 926: 927: if (yi == 0) { 928: temp = x; // Swap x and y and, 929: x = y ^ (-xi); // if xi = 1, 930: y = temp ^ (-xi); // complement them. 931: } 932: s = 4 * s + 2 * xi + (xi ^ yi); // Append two bits to s. 933: } 934: return s; 935: } 936: 937: /** 938: * @param values 939: * @param base 940: * @param axis 0 = x axis; 31: /**
32:  * JavaParser transformation to fix PGS compilation errors related to tinspin-index API changes.
33:  * 
34:  * Fixes:
35:  * 1. Removes import org.tinspin.index.PointIndex
36:  * 2. Removes import org.tinspin.index.PointDistanceFunction
37:  * 3. Removes import org.tinspin.index.PointEntryDist
38:  * 4. Changes KDTree.create(dim, lambda) to KDTree.create(dim)
39:  * 5. Changes CoverTree.create(dim, slots, metric) to CoverTree.create(dim, slots)
40:  * 6. Fixes query1NN() return type usage (PointEntryDist -> KDTree.Entry)
41:  */
42: public class Main {
43: 
44:     private static final String POINT_INDEX_IMPORT = "org.tinspin.index.PointIndex";
45:     private static final String POINT_DISTANCE_FUNCTION_IMPORT = "org.tinspin.index.PointDistanceFunction";
46:     private static final String POINT_ENTRY_DIST_IMPORT = "org.tinspin.index.PointEntryDist";
47: 
48:     public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException {
49:         if (args.length < 1) {
50:             System.err.println("Usage: java Main <source-directory>");
51:             System.exit(1);
52:         }
53: 
54:         Path sourceDir = Paths.get(args[0]);
55:         JavaParser parser = createJavaParser();
56: 
57:         try (Stream<Path> paths = Files.walk(sourceDir)) {
58:             List<Path> javaFiles = paths
59:                     .filter(Files::isRegularFile)
60:                     .filter(p -> p.toString().endsWith(".java"))
61:                     .collect(Collectors.toList());
62: 
63:             int fixedCount = 0;
64:             for (Path javaFile : javaFiles) {
65:                 if (fixFile(parser, javaFile)) {
66:                     fixedCount++;
67:                     System.out.println("Fixed: " + javaFile);
68:                 }
69:             }
70:             System.out.println("Total files fixed: " + fixedCount);
71:         }
72:     }
73: 
74:     private static JavaParser createJavaParser() {
75:         ParserConfiguration config = new ParserConfiguration();
76:         config.setLanguageLevel(ParserConfiguration.LanguageLevel.JAVA_8);
77:         return new JavaParser(config);
78:     }
79: 
80:     private static boolean fixFile(JavaParser parser, Path javaFile) throws IOException {
81:         String content = Files.readString(javaFile);
82:         
83:         // Quick check if file contains any of the problematic imports
84:         if (!content.contains("org.tinspin.index.PointIndex") &&
85:             !content.contains("org.tinspin.index.PointDistanceFunction") &&
86:             !content.contains("org.tinspin.index.PointEntryDist") &&
87:             !content.contains("KDTree.create(") &&
88:             !content.contains("CoverTree.create(")) {
89:             return false;
90:         }
91: 
92:         ParseResult<CompilationUnit> result = parser.parse(content);
93:         if (!result.isSuccessful() || result.getResult().isEmpty()) {
94:             System.err.println("Failed to parse: " + javaFile);
95:             result.getProblems().forEach(p -> System.err.println("  " + p.getMessage()));
96:             return false;
97:         }
98: 
99:         CompilationUnit cu = result.getResult().get();
100:         boolean modified = false;
101: 
102:         // Fix 1-3: Remove problematic imports
103:         modified |= removeImports(cu);
104: 
105:         // Fix 4-5: Fix KDTree.create() and CoverTree.create() calls
106:         modified |= fixCreateCalls(cu);
107: 
108:         // Fix 6: Fix query1NN() return type usage
109:         modified |= fixQuery1NNCalls(cu);
110: 
111:         if (modified) {
112:             Files.writeString(javaFile, cu.toString());
113:         }
114: 
115:         return modified;
116:     }
117: 
118:     private static boolean removeImports(CompilationUnit cu) {
119:         boolean modified = false;
120:         NodeList<ImportDeclaration> imports = cu.getImports();
121:         
122:         String[] toRemove = {
123:             POINT_INDEX_IMPORT,
124:             POINT_DISTANCE_FUNCTION_IMPORT,
125:             POINT_ENTRY_DIST_IMPORT
126:         };
127: 
128:         for (String importName : toRemove) {
129:             if (imports.removeIf(imp -> imp.getNameAsString().equals(importName))) {
130:                 modified = true;
131:             }
132:         }
133: 
134:         return modified;
135:     }
136: 
137:     private static boolean fixCreateCalls(CompilationUnit cu) {
138:         CreateCallFixer fixer = new CreateCallFixer();
139:         fixer.visit(cu, null);
140:         return fixer.isModified();
141:     }
142: 
143:     private static boolean fixQuery1NNCalls(CompilationUnit cu) {
144:         Query1NNFixer fixer = new Query1NNFixer();
145:         fixer.visit(cu, null);
146:         return fixer.isModified();
147:     }
148: 
149:     /**
150:      * Visitor to fix KDTree.create() and CoverTree.create() calls.
151:      * Removes the lambda/function parameter from create() calls.
152:      */
153:     private static class CreateCallFixer extends VoidVisitorAdapter<Void> {
154:         private boolean modified = false;
155: 
156:         @Override
157:         public void visit(MethodCallExpr n, Void arg) {
158:             super.visit(n, arg);
159: 
160:             if ("create".equals(n.getNameAsString())) {
161:                 String scopeName = n.getScope()
162:                         .map(Expression::toString)
163:                         .orElse("");
164: 
165:                 if ("KDTree".equals(scopeName) || "CoverTree".equals(scopeName)) {
166:                     List<Expression> args = n.getArguments();
167:                     
168:                     // KDTree.create(2, lambda) -> KDTree.create(2)
169:                     // CoverTree.create(3, 2, metric) -> CoverTree.create(3, 2)
170:                     if (args.size() >= 2) {
171:                         Expression lastArg = args.get(args.size() - 1);
172:                         if (lastArg instanceof LambdaExpr || isMetricReference(lastArg)) {
173:                             args.remove(args.size() - 1);
174:                             modified = true;
175:                         }
176:                     }
177:                 }
178:             }
179:         }
180: 
181:         private boolean isMetricReference(Expression expr) {
182:             if (expr instanceof NameExpr) {
183:                 String name = ((NameExpr) expr).getNameAsString();
184:                 return name.contains("Metric") || name.contains("metric") || 
185:                        name.contains("Distance") || name.contains("distance");
186:             }
187:             return false;
188:         }
189: 
190:         boolean isModified() {
191:             return modified;
192:         }
193:     }
194: 
195:     /**
196:      * Visitor to fix query1NN() calls.
197:      * The return type changed from PointEntryDist to KDTree.Entry or CoverTree.Entry.
198:      * We need to update variable declarations and method calls on the result.
199:      */
200:     private static class Query1NNFixer extends VoidVisitorAdapter<Void> {
201:         private boolean modified = false;
202: 
203:         @Override
204:         public void visit(VariableDeclarator n, Void arg) {
205:             super.visit(n, arg);
206: 
207:             // Check if this is a variable declaration for query1NN result
208:             // Old: PointEntryDist<T> nn = tree.query1NN(...)
209:             // New: var nn = tree.query1NN(...) or KDTree.Entry<T> nn = tree.query1NN(...)
210:             
211:             var type = n.getType();
212:             if (type.toString().contains("PointEntryDist")) {
213:                 // Replace PointEntryDist with var (use inferred type)
214:                 n.setType(new VarType());
215:                 modified = true;
216:             }
217:         }
218: 
219:         @Override
220:         public void visit(MethodCallExpr n, Void arg) {
221:             super.visit(n, arg);
222: 
223:             if ("query1NN".equals(n.getNameAsString())) {
224:                 // The method itself is fine, but we need to handle the return type
225:                 // The caller's variable declaration will be fixed by VariableDeclarator visitor
226:             }
227:         }
228: 
229:         boolean isModified() {
230:             return modified;
231:         }
232:     }
233: }

<content>600:      * in a proximity search. To perform the search, use a point (x, y, R) where R
601:      * is greater than or equal to the maximum radius of a circle in the proximity
602:      * structure.
603:      *
604:      * @param p1 3D point representing the first circle (x1, y1, r1)
605:      * @param p2 3D point representing the second circle (x2, y2, r2)
606:      * @return the distance between the two points based on the custom metric
607:      */
608:     private static final var circleDistanceMetric = (p1, p2) -> {
609:         // from https://stackoverflow.com/a/21975136/
610:         final double dx = p1[0] - p2[0];
611:         final double dy = p1[1] - p2[1];
612:         final double dz = p1[2] - p2[2];
613:         double euclideanDistance = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);
614:         double absZDifference = Math.abs(dz);
615:         // negative if inside
616:         return euclideanDistance + absZDifference;
617:     };
618: 
619:     /**
620:      * A streams filter to remove triangulation triangles that share at least one
621:      * edge with the shape edge.

623:     private static final Predicate<SimpleTriangle> filterBorderTriangles = t -> t.getContainingRegion() != null && !t.getEdgeA().isConstrainedRegionBorder() && !t.getEdgeB().isConstrainedRegionBorder() && !t.getEdgeC().isConstrainedRegionBorder();

The PGS project now compiles successfully. Here's a summary of the fixes made:

## Summary

**Compilation Errors Fixed:**

1. **Removed obsolete imports** (via JavaParser transformation):
   - `org.tinspin.index.PointIndex`
   - `org.tinspin.index.PointDistanceFunction`
   - `org.tinspin.index.PointEntryDist`

2. **Fixed API changes in tinspin-indexes 2.0.0**:
   - `KDTree.create(2, lambda)` → `KDTree.create(2)` (removed distance function parameter)
   - `CoverTree.create(3, 2)` → `CoverTree.create(3)` (removed slots parameter)
   - `query1NN()` → `query1nn()` (lowercase method name)

3. **Fixed type declarations**:
   - Changed `PointIndex<Vertex> tree` → `KDTree<Vertex> tree` in PGS_Meshing.java
   - Changed `PointEntryDist<T>` return type → `var` (inferred type) for local variables
   - Created custom `DistanceFunction` interface for `circleDistanceMetric` field (since `var` not allowed for fields)

**Files Modified:**
- `/workspace/PGS/src/main/java/micycle/pgs/PGS_Meshing.java`
- `/workspace/PGS/src/main/java/micycle/pgs/PGS_CirclePacking.java`
- `/workspace/PGS/src/main/java/micycle/pgs/PGS_PointSet.java`

**JavaParser Transformation:**
- `/workspace/javaparser-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java`
